mission.title: argo: Please allow for defining dependency DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph)
mission.description: Please allow for defining dependency DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph). Requested by 132+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
helm list -n argo 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo installation.

steps.title: Review argo configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get configmap -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo
```
When defining Application please allow a new field that is very similar to SyncWaves but allows for more fine-grained control of dependencies, thus allowing more parallelism.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Please allow for defining dependency DAG (Directed Acyclic
steps.description:
PoC for #3517 - up for discussion. Things to note:

* Currently, manual syncing is also blocked when there are unmet dependencies
* Not a single test yet

Enables to specify dependencies between `Applications`:

```
dependsOn:
  - applicationName: otherApp
  - applicationName: someApp
```
```yaml
syncWaves:
    clusterBootstrap:
      namespaces:
        platformTopology: 0
        playgroundTopology: 0
      priorityClasses: 0
      storageClasses: 0
      sealedSecretsController: 10
      sealedSecrets: 20
      spotTerminationHandler: 10
    istio:
      controlPlane: 1000
      kiali: 1010
    infra:
      jaeger: 2000
      prometheus:
        operator: 2000
        alertRules: 2010
        grafanaDashboard: 2010
        thanos: 2010
      certManager: 2000
      argoCd: 2100
    operators:
      spark: 3000
      zookeeper: 3000
    knative:
      operat
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Please allow for defining dependency DAG (Directed Acyclic" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
PoC for #3517 - up for discussion. Things to note:

* Currently, manual syncing is also blocked when there are unmet dependencies
* Not a single test yet

Enables to specify dependencies between `Applications`:

```
dependsOn:
  - applicationName: otherApp
  - applicationName: someApp
```
codeSnippets:
syncWaves:
    clusterBootstrap:
      namespaces:
        platformTopology: 0
        playgroundTopology: 0
      priorityClasses: 0
      storageClasses: 0
      sealedSecretsController: 10
      sealedSecrets: 20
      spotTerminationHandler: 10
    istio:
      controlPlane: 1000
      kiali: 1010
    infra:
      jaeger: 2000
      prometheus:
        operator: 2000
        alertRules: 2010
        grafanaDashboard: 2010
        thanos: 2010
      certManager: 2000
      argoCd: 2100
    operators:
      spark: 3000
      zookeeper: 3000
    knative:
      operator: 3000
      serving: 4000
      eventing: 4000
      source:
        config: 3000
        sqs: 4000
      kafka:
        zookeeper: 4000
    search:
      core:
        zookeeper: 5000

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
